records.ety: [OE. skie a cloud, Icel. sk; akin to Sw. & Dan. sky; cf. AS. scūa, scūwa, shadow, Icel. skuggi; probably from the same root as E. scum. √158. See Scum, and cf. Hide skin, Obscure.]

quotes: [A wind] that blew so hideously and high, That it ne lefte not a sky In all the welkin long and broad.
She passeth as it were a sky.

defs: The apparent arch, or vault, of heaven, which in a clear day is of a blue color; the heavens; the firmament; -- sometimes in the plural.
quotes: The Norweyan banners flout the sky.
Thou wert better in thy grave than to answer with thy uncovered body this extremity of the skies.
notes: ☞ Sky is often used adjectively or in the formation of self-explaining compounds; as, sky color, skylight, sky-aspiring, sky-born, sky-pointing, sky-roofed, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Skied (?) or Skyed; p. pr. & vb. n. Skying (?).]

defs: To hang (a picture on exhibition) near the top of a wall, where it can not be well seen.
quotes: Brother Academicians who skied his pictures.

defs: To throw towards the sky; as, to sky a ball at cricket.
